machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase; disassembles and recycles the contracted sheath
machinery_genes.quote: we generated a B. thai strain bearing an in-frame clpV-1 deletion, and a strain in which this deletion was complemented by clpV-1 expression from a neutral site on the chromosome. In plate competition assays, the Δ clpV-1 strain displayed a fitness defect similar to ΔT6SS-1, and clpV-1 expression complemented the phenotype
machinery_genes.note: Strongest instance-tied call: clpV-1 (BTH_I2958, given verbatim in the Methods: 'the conserved T6SS genes tssK-5 (BTH_II0857) and clpV-1 (BTH_I2958) were deleted') is the ATPase of B. thailandensis T6SS-1; its in-frame deletion phenocopies the whole-T6SS-1 deletion and is complemented. Independently confirmed as the T6SS-1 ATPase by Lennings 2019: 'ClpV-1-GFP of the Burkholderia T6SS-1, which targets prokaryotic cells'. The brief instructs counting ClpV (TssH).

machinery_genes.quote: B. thai and E. cloacae strains bearing in-frame deletions of the tssM genes associated with their known antibacterial T6S pathways, are unable to stimulate T6S in P. aeruginosa
machinery_genes.note: B. thailandensis' known antibacterial T6S pathway is T6SS-1 (Schwarz 2010). The deletion strain is referred to throughout the paper's figures as 'B. thai ΔtssM-1', i.e. the TssM of the T6SS-1 cluster. TssM is a core membrane-complex structural component of the T6S apparatus.

machinery_genes.role: secreted hexameric inner tube; co-dependent extracellular component of the apparatus
machinery_genes.quote: Two proteins exported by the T6SS are haemolysin co-regulated protein (Hcp) and valine-glycine repeat protein G (VgrG). Secretion of these proteins is co-dependent, and they may be extracellular components of the apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core exported component of the T6S apparatus in the founding B. thailandensis T6SS paper (which characterizes the five B. thai systems and assigns T6SS-1 the antibacterial role). The quote names Hcp at the level of 'the T6SS' apparatus rather than singling out the cluster-1 hcp gene; per the brief, Hcp is counted as machinery (secreted tube). Medium confidence: family-level naming, not a cluster-1-specific locus quote.

machinery_genes.role: secreted trimeric spike at the tube tip; co-dependent extracellular component of the apparatus
machinery_genes.quote: Two proteins exported by the T6SS are haemolysin co-regulated protein (Hcp) and valine-glycine repeat protein G (VgrG). Secretion of these proteins is co-dependent, and they may be extracellular components of the apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core exported component of the T6S apparatus in the founding B. thailandensis T6SS paper. Per the brief, VgrG is counted as machinery (spike). The same paper separately names the T6SS-5-specific VgrG-5 (Schwarz 2014, IAI), confirming each B. thai cluster carries its own vgrG; the cluster-1 vgrG (vgrG-1) is the spike of T6SS-1. Medium confidence: family-level naming, not a cluster-1-specific locus quote. Note: the VgrG/PAAR-fused C-terminal toxin domains delivered to targets are effectors, not machinery, and are excluded.

machinery_genes.role: contractile sheath subunit (inner); forms remodelable tubules with VipB
machinery_genes.quote: ClpV-interacting proteins A and B (VipA and VipB) form tubules that are remodeled by the ATPase, which could indicate a role for the protein in secretion system biogenesis
machinery_genes.note: VipA = TssB, the inner sheath subunit. The founding paper built its T6S phylogeny on the B. thai VipA orthologs ('the systems of B. thai... 334 T6S-associated VipA orthologs'; 'Locus tag numbers are based on B. thai E264 genome annotations'), so VipA is named as a B. thailandensis T6S apparatus component. Family-level naming; the cluster-1 copy (vipA-1/tssB-1) is the sheath of T6SS-1. Medium confidence.

machinery_genes.quote: ClpV-interacting proteins A and B (VipA and VipB) form tubules that are remodeled by the ATPase, which could indicate a role for the protein in secretion system biogenesis
machinery_genes.note: VipB = TssC, the outer sheath subunit, named alongside VipA as a conserved B. thailandensis T6S apparatus component. Family-level naming; the cluster-1 copy is the outer sheath of T6SS-1. Medium confidence.

notes: System: B. thailandensis E264 encodes five T6SS clusters; T6SS-1 (the sys_instance_label here) is the antibacterial, contact-dependent interbacterial-competition system, established by Schwarz et al 2010 PLoS Pathog ('T6SS-1 is involved in cell contact-dependent interbacterial interactions'; 'we generated a B. thai strain bearing an in-frame clpV-1 deletion... similar to ΔT6SS-1'). The five curated effectors (BTH_I0070, BTH_I2691-I2694) are delivered toxins, not machinery, and are excluded. Why PARTIAL, not COMPLETE: only two apparatus genes are tied to the cluster-1 instance by an explicit gene-level quote in the published literature: clpV-1/TssH (Schwarz 2010, with locus tag BTH_I2958; re-confirmed as the T6SS-1 ATPase by Lennings 2019) and tssM-1/TssM (LeRoux 2015, 'B. thai ΔtssM-1'). The secreted tube/spike (Hcp/TssD, VgrG/TssI) and the contractile sheath (VipA/TssB, VipB/TssC) are named as components of the B. thailandensis T6S apparatus in the founding paper, but at the level of 'the T6SS' apparatus family rather than with cluster-1-specific naming quotes; they are included because the brief mandates counting Hcp, VgrG and the sheath as machinery, and each B. thai cluster carries its own copy (cf. the cluster-5-specific VgrG-5, Schwarz 2014). Deliberately NOT listed (no defensible naming quote tying them to B. thai T6SS-1): baseplate genes TssE/TssF/TssG, baseplate-membrane link TssK, the IM component TssL, the OM lipoprotein TssJ, the cap protein TssA, and any PAAR protein. These almost certainly exist in the BTH_I2954-I2968 cluster (annotation-derivable) but I found no published sentence naming the cluster-1 copies, and the hard rules forbid mining the GenBank/automated detectors to discover them. Strain transfer: none required; all apparatus quotes are from B. thailandensis E264 itself except the role vocabulary (TssB/TssC/TssD/TssH/TssI/TssM mapping) taken from the apparatus brief. Lowest-confidence calls: Hcp, VgrG, VipA, VipB (family-level, not locus-level, quotes). According to PubMed, sources: Schwarz et al 2010 (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.ppat.1001068), LeRoux et al 2015 (https://doi.org/10.7554/eLife.05701), Lennings et al 2019 (https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ijmm.2019.151335).
